front: Work: a character born from a stone egg becomes king of the monkeys after jumping through a waterfall at Flower Fruit Mountain
Work: a character loses a bet after being unable to somersault out of a hand; he has seventy-two transformations
Work: a character obtains a shape-changing iron staff from the Dragon King of the Eastern Sea (Ao Guang), whose palace is guarded by shrimps and crabs
Work: a character is captured when hit in the head by a bracelet thrown from heaven during a fight
Work: a character angers his master Subodhi by transforming into a pine tree; Subodhi named him for his awareness of the vacuity of the universe
Work: a character journeys to the Chinese underworld and meets the Ten Yama Kings at the capital Youdu
Work: a character who uses a nine-toothed rake is tasked with cleaning altars; he is a demoted demigod
Work: the daughter of a village elder is kidnapped by her hard-working suitor who consumes more food than the farm produces
Work: demons attempt to eat a monk's flesh multiple times throughout a pilgrimage to India
Work: the monk rides a white horse who is actually a dragon
Work: in a 17th-century supplement, characters experience bizarre dreams by a 'fish spirit of sexual desire'; one character can time travel through a fish demon's dream world
work: The Supplement to Journey to the West and Other Episodes

front: Work: a character turns into a fly and enters a demon's body to obtain a banana leaf fan needed to pass flaming mountains
work: The Supplement to Journey to the West and Other Episodes

front: Work: Arthur Waley's abridged translation of this 100-chapter Chinese novel covers only 30 chapters and is titled Monkey
Work: based on the historical monk Xuanzang's 7th-century pilgrimage to India, documented in the Great Tang Records on the Western Regions
Work: the destination of the pilgrimage is Vulture Peak in India, where the characters seek Buddhist scriptures
Work: a Chinese aphorism states 'when you're young, you shouldn't read' this work, quoted in Xuan Juliana Wang's Home Remedies
